authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal Medicare program administrator. The Medicare Claims Processing Manual is the official public source for claim submission, processing, payment, adjustment, remittance, correction, and filing workflows that constrain healthcare billing operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Intake, claim support validation, submission, posting, and correction handling
used_for: billing, payment, adjustment, and correction workflow boundaries
refusal when requested billing or closeout action lacks authoritative claim evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Billing: CMS-1500 and 837P
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This CMS MLN booklet directly governs professional-claim form use, electronic 837P submission, provider and place-of-service facts, filing posture, and documentation dependencies for provider-side claim readiness.
used_for: provider, payer, and place-of-service prerequisite checks
claim-readiness output shape and timely-filing awareness

authoritative_sources.title: National Correct Coding Initiative Edits
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS maintains NCCI edit policy for Medicare and Medicaid claim integrity. It is authoritative for recognizing coding-adjacent reimbursement edit dependencies while preserving the boundary that this lane cannot assign final codes or modifiers.
used_for: routing to coding coordination when code, modifier, or documentation interpretation is required
refusal to make final coding decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Prior Authorization and Pre-Claim Review Initiatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization and pre-claim review guidance is an official payer operating reference for services where approval or review status gates claim submission and where missing documentation must block confident billing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, pre-claim, and documentation prerequisite handling
used_for: claim-readiness holds when approval status is missing
routing to patient access, authorization, or utilization owners

authoritative_sources.title: Health Care Payment and Remittance Advice and Electronic Funds Transfer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administrative simplification guidance is authoritative for ERA and EFT concepts, payment-to-remittance reassociation, CARC and RARC usage, and healthcare remittance handling in provider revenue workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Remittance, payment posting, denial, and reconciliation handling

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Secondary Payer and Coordination of Benefits
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS coordination of benefits and Medicare Secondary Payer guidance is the public payer baseline for determining when another payer may be primary and when claim, remittance, or patient-balance handling must stop for COB or MSP review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: COB, secondary payer, and patient-balance exception handling
used_for: patient-balance hold conditions when payer responsibility is unresolved

authoritative_sources.title: First Level of Appeal: Redetermination by a Medicare Contractor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS redetermination guidance is an official payer workflow source for when an operational denial moves to formal appeal ownership, documentation expectations, MAC handling, and the boundary between minor correction and appeal.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Denial escalation and formal appeal boundary
used_for: refusal to certify payer appeal arguments

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are recognized national healthcare administrative workflow rules for eligibility, claim status, payment, and remittance transactions and are used to normalize payer-provider transaction expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, claim status, payment, and remittance transaction workflow

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is authoritative for HIPAA Privacy Rule guidance. This source constrains healthcare billing requests by distinguishing payment and operations uses from disclosures requiring privacy review.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI access, use, and disclosure in payment and operations workflows and directly constrains billing, posting, denial, and escalation packets.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Payer, plan, active coverage, eligibility, referral, authorization or pre-claim status, place of service, rendering and billing provider facts, claim type, timely-filing posture, COB or MSP payer hierarchy, encounter documentation, charge ticket support, and tenant approval thresholds gate billing, posting, adjustment, or closeout actions.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: The truth set is established by EHR encounter and charge capture records, practice-management registration and patient-account ledgers, eligibility and coverage records, authorization and referral repositories, claim scrubber and clearinghouse files, payer portal and claim-status responses, 835 ERA, EFT, EOB or remittance records, denial-management work queues, adjustment logs, approval systems, and document repositories.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at administrative evidence sufficiency, root-cause categorization, and handoff packaging. Licensed clinical or coding owners decide diagnoses, procedures, modifiers, medical necessity, treatment planning, and medical-record changes; privacy or compliance owners decide non-routine disclosure and PHI disputes; payer-policy, appeal, legal, accounting, and compliance owners decide formal interpretation, appeal certification, reimbursement policy, and unsupported financial changes.

workflow_stages.name: Charge, billing, and financial intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify patient or member, encounter, payer, plan, place of service, provider facts, claim type, requested financial action, charge ticket, documentation status, coverage, eligibility, referral, authorization, timely-filing, and COB or MSP dependencies before work proceeds.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Claim-readiness and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate that encounter documentation, charge support, authorization or referral evidence, coverage, payer hierarchy, code-support artifacts, provider facts, claim form requirements, and tenant approval rules support claim generation or correction.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Submission, status, posting, and reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Track the claim through claim scrubber, clearinghouse, payer, and claim-status channels, then relate ERA, EFT, EOB, remittance, posting batch, and patient-ledger records to the correct financial outcome.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Denial, variance, and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare EHR, practice-management, billing, clearinghouse, payer portal, remittance, denial-management, and approval records to identify matched items, missing documentation, authorization defects, COB mismatches, denials, underpayments, unsupported adjustments, and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation for unsupported adjustments or interpretation questions
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when code selection, clinical documentation, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, formal appeal, privacy approval, accounting treatment, legal advice, refund, write-off, waiver, or unsupported financial change authority is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout and evidence retention
workflow_stages.description: Close only when billing, posting, reconciliation, denial, adjustment, COB, approval, and PHI-minimum evidence is present, linked to source records, and unresolved blockers are assigned to the correct owner.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: charge tickets, superbills, charge capture records, and claim edit work items
encounter notes, signed orders, clinical documentation status, and coding-support artifacts
coverage, eligibility, referral, authorization, pre-claim, COB, MSP, and patient-responsibility records
payer, plan, place-of-service, rendering provider, billing provider, NPI, taxonomy, and provider enrollment facts
CMS-1500, UB-04, 837P, 837I, claim-scrubber output, clearinghouse acknowledgments, and payer portal claim-status records
835 ERA files, EFT detail, remittance advice, EOBs, CARC and RARC details, denial letters, and payer correspondence
practice-management ledger activity, patient statements, cash-posting batches, adjustment logs, refund requests, write-off requests, and variance worksheets
denial-management inventory, appeal-prep notes, owner routing notes, approval matrices, and audit logs
tenant payer-policy extracts, state Medicaid or commercial payer rules, MAC articles, privacy policies, and system precedence records
systems_of_record: registration, eligibility, referral, and authorization systems
payer and Medicare Administrative Contractor portals
ERA, EFT, remittance, EOB, and payment posting systems
document repositories for payer correspondence and authorization support
approval, adjustment, refund, write-off, and audit-log systems
tenant policy, payer contract, and provider enrollment repositories

allowed: classify healthcare billing requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or requiring adjacent escalation
determine whether retrieved source records support claim readiness, posting, reconciliation, denial categorization, or exception packaging
identify missing documentation, eligibility, coverage, referral, authorization, COB, timely-filing, remittance, posting, or approval prerequisites
categorize denials and variances by likely root-cause class without assigning final codes, making clinical judgments, or interpreting payer policy
prepare claim-readiness summaries, remittance and denial reconciliation notes, unsupported financial-change refusals, and escalation packets
escalate: missing payer, plan, place of service, authorization status, filing limit, denial inventory context, source record, or system path would change the outcome
EHR, practice-management, billing, clearinghouse, payer portal, remittance, patient-ledger, or approval records materially disagree

coding, medical necessity, clinical documentation, payer-policy interpretation, formal appeal, privacy, compliance, accounting, tax, legal, refund, write-off, waiver, or executive authority is required
COB, MSP, patient-balance, denial, or collection handling could create patient or member harm without human review
refuse: requests to assign final codes, rewrite or fabricate medical records, determine medical necessity, or imply treatment planning or licensed clinical judgment
requests to submit, post, adjust, waive, write off, refund, or close without source evidence and delegated authority
requests to disclose PHI outside approved minimum-necessary payment or operations handling
requests to resolve conflicting public guidance, payer rules, tenant policy, or system records through confident synthesis instead of escalation

domain_failure_modes: submitting a claim because a charge exists even though encounter documentation, authorization, referral, coverage, or provider facts do not support billing
treating coding-adjacent NCCI or payer edits as permission for the billing lane to assign codes, modifiers, or medical necessity conclusions
forcing an ERA, EOB, EFT, payer portal, posting batch, and patient ledger to tie without preserving unresolved variance evidence
writing off, waiving, refunding, reclassifying, or changing patient responsibility without policy support, approval evidence, and traceable source records
disclosing more PHI than the minimum necessary for payment or operations, or sending billing packets outside approved channels
mistaking eligibility, authorization, or payer phone-note status for final payer-policy interpretation
posting a patient balance when COB, MSP, secondary payer, or payer hierarchy records conflict
collapsing Medicare, Medicaid, Medicare Advantage, commercial, workers compensation, professional, institutional, and secondary-payer paths into one generic revenue rule
missing patient or member harm from bad routing, missing escalation, incorrect assumptions, or premature collections activity
omitting an audit trail that downstream coding, appeal, compliance, privacy, accounting, or payer-policy owners need

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A charge is present but documentation or authorization is insufficient to bill
A payer denial requires correct routing between coding, access, and billing owners
A user asks to force a financial adjustment not supported by policy or evidence
Mismatch between source record and financial outcome
Low-confidence or missing tenant facts and systems
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific rules and state Medicaid variation
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public CMS sources do not resolve every Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, commercial, workers compensation, state, MAC, or tenant contract rule. Runtime must retrieve payer-plan and tenant-specific rules before applying the baseline to a specific claim.
unresolved_ambiguities.mitigation: The pack requires payer, plan, jurisdiction, payer-policy, filing limit, and tenant policy retrieval before confident case handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: NCCI and payer edit awareness can identify that coding or medical necessity matters, but the billing lane cannot decide final codes, modifiers, diagnoses, documentation wording, or medical necessity.
unresolved_ambiguities.mitigation: Scenarios hbs-05, hbs-08, and hbs-10 require routing to coding, clinical documentation, utilization, payer-policy, or appeal owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: PHI disclosure and minimum necessary handling
unresolved_ambiguities.description: HIPAA permits some payment and healthcare-operations uses, but non-routine disclosures, unusual channels, or unclear requester authority require tenant privacy controls and designated privacy owner review.
unresolved_ambiguities.mitigation: The pack refuses uncontrolled disclosure and requires minimum-necessary packets and privacy escalation.
